description: Corresponding exception flag is set for all exceptions, interrupts and NMIs in Table 3.
example_target: All exceptions, interrupts, NMIs in Table 3
notes: See Table 3 for full list: Reset, NMI types, Async interrupts, instruction-fetch exceptions, decode exceptions, execution exceptions, load/store exceptions.

description: mtval is written with exception-specific information to assist software in handling the trap, corresponding to the exception, interrupt or NMI.
mcause is set to the exception code corresponding to the exception, interrupt or NMI.
When a trap is taken, previous privilege level for machine mode (mpp bit of mstatus) is set to privilege level before exception or interrupt.
Interrupt enable bit for machine mode (mie) set to 0 when a trap is taken.
Previous interrupt enable bit for machine mode (mpie) set to value of previous mie.
For exception, exception program counter for machine mode (mepc) set to PC from WB stage. For interrupt, mepc has to be set to current PC value.
By executing MRET when mpp=0, modify privilege bit mprv=0 and mpp=0.
notes: Sub-property of 491-498 (MRET update status). MRET returns from a trap in machine mode.

description: 1 is set to mpie when the state is returned to the previous state before the occurrence of a trap due to the MRET instruction.
The value held in mpie is restored in mie when the state is returned to the previous state before the occurrence of a trap due to the MRET instruction.
If an exception appeared, exception handler should be started, if conditions for exception start are fulfilled.
Exceptions are not being handled in absence of any request for exception.

Interrupt implies handled. When interrupt happens, interrupt_counter increases by 1 and eventually interrupt service routine occurs. interrupt_counter is decreased for every interrupt service routine start.
notes: Uses strong eventuality operator for liveness checking.

description: Interrupt is disabled/enabled according to value of mie bit. Interrupt taken is enabled when mie bit is high and disabled when mie bit is low.
The Interrupt bit in mcause is set if the trap was caused by an interrupt.
mepc register is written with the address of the instruction that was interrupted.
If frm is set to an invalid value (101 to 111), any subsequent attempt to execute a floating-point operation with a dynamic rounding mode is illegal.
